mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability through higher borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04 in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months with declining PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration of retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 700,727 million and HOUST at 1,331 thousand as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-decline instances over any 2-year rolling window, or interaction effect (joint decline) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) relative to single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in industrial production below the 102.0 index level signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 102.0 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 102.0 over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.05 in supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth above 0.4% combined with elevated FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals persistent monetary tightness that constrains equity valuations through both higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 311.054 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-02-15
regime_hint: stagflation or persistent inflation with tight monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-trigger instances over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.2% signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and consumer confidence, allowing the Federal Reserve to pause or cut rates, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.2% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle softening with monetary policy pivot
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.2% over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04 in labor-slack regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings expansion and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,668.986 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.05 in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST declining below 1,300 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,300 thousand for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 1,300 thousand over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03 in housing-constrained regimes.
